title: Speed and quality of complex strategic decisions
journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

title: Global trends in robot-assisted ureteral reimplantation: a bibliometric and visualization analysis
reasoning_research: The article is a bibliometric analysis of a surgical technique (robot-assisted ureteral reimplantation) with no connection to decision neuroscience, computational psychology, or self-control/attention research. The methods are standard bibliometric tools, not experimental or modeling approaches relevant to the field. There is no conceptual novelty or methodological rigor pertaining to decision-making processes.
reasoning_impact: The findings are specific to urology and have no relevance to self-control, attention, reinforcement learning, or any core topic in decision neuroscience. The work cannot influence future research directions in computational psychology or behavioral decision-making. Its implications are confined to a narrow surgical specialty.

title: Humanoid Robot Walking and Grasping Method Using Similarity Reward-Augmented Generative Adversarial Imitation Learning
